title: The Evolution of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: The Evolution of Evangelion             Quote Post by C.W. Smith » Tue Feb 19, 2013 1:18 am I just recently decided to try and get back into writing fanfiction for Evangelion, starting with an Eva/Harry Potter crossover. The thread for that is in the Fanfiction forum if anyone is interested in looking at it. But I got to thinking. Evangelion, the original series, was first broadcast back in the mid 90s. I didn't really learn of it until 2001. And it became a bit of an obsession with me, got me into fanfiction and actually doing some series web development. Had to get others who knew more as time went on as I had other obligations. Now we have the Rebuild of Evangelion, the theatrical reboot of the series. I haven't had a chance, or the spare cash, to purchase any beyond 1.01 but I'm HOPING to be able to start purchasing them here shortly. Thing is, and I've been wondering, how has this series, and our interpretation of it evolved over almost two decades of the series existence? How have we inturpretted the characters, become emotionally invested in them, and tried to come to an understanding not only of them, but of the themes, concepts, and basic human stories they represent?

[]:             Quote Post by Yossarian Dies » Thu Feb 21, 2013 9:10 pm I don't get what you're asking. Are you asking if we feel different about the series than we did previously?

Article: The Evolution of Evangelion
[]: The Evolution of Evangelion             Quote Post by C.W. Smith » Tue Feb 19, 2013 1:18 am I just recently decided to try and get back into writing fanfiction for Evangelion, starting with an Eva/Harry Potter crossover. The thread for that is in the Fanfiction forum if anyone is interested in looking at it. But I got to thinking. Evangelion, the original series, was first broadcast back in the mid 90s. I didn't really learn of it until 2001. And it became a bit of an obsession with me, got me into fanfiction and actually doing some series web development. Had to get others who knew more as time went on as I had other obligations. Now we have the Rebuild of Evangelion, the theatrical reboot of the series. I haven't had a chance, or the spare cash, to purchase any beyond 1.01 but I'm HOPING to be able to start purchasing them here shortly. Thing is, and I've been wondering, how has this series, and our interpretation of it evolved over almost two decades of the series existence? How have we inturpretted the characters, become emotionally invested in them, and tried to come to an understanding not only of them, but of the themes, concepts, and basic human stories they represent?
